reviews.review:
Absolutely useless. The little tubs that hold the liquid are made of the
thinnest of plastic, and every single tub was warped, causing it to sit
lopsided in the hard case. I spilled some liquid on my hands, and
honestly, it just smells like pure vinegar. I mean, maybe there's some
sort of pheromone or chemical in there but it's not effective. Every
summer, the flies are so bad in my area. I've had these tubs sitting on
my windowsills for three days now. I placed them there because that's
where a lot of the flies gravitate to, but it hasn't caught a single
one. Save your money, and invest in a good flyswatter. I suggest a metal
one. Oh, and stay away from those little zapping paddles because I tried
that too. All it did was knock the fly out for literally five seconds.
Hardly enough time to finish the job before they regain their senses and
fly away.

I bought these traps to put in my kitchen and bathrooms. In our kitchen,
we tend to get fruit flies around our coffee machine. In the bathroom,
we get them around the sinks, as they come out of the overflow drains
and like to hang around my potted plants.

These come in a 6-pack, which includes 6 small plastic cases and 6
single-use solution tubs.

To setup, you take on plastic case, and one solution tub. Peel the
sealed cover off of the solution tub, drop it into the case, and put the
lid on the case. From there, you just place it in the area where you
notice flying insects.

The solution looks unremarkable, visually, but smells slightly of
vinegar. After these had dried up, it looked somewhat goopy, but still
smelled of vinegar. Putting a bit of water into the solution tub quickly
reconstitutes the solution. Given this, I believe the solution is
nothing more than vinegar, water, and soap, which is the recipe for a
DIY fruit fly trap, but in a MUCH smaller scale.

The first night I put these out, I caught a single fruit fly in the
bathroom, which made me very hopeful that these were to solution to my
problem. Unfortunately, these seem to be absolutely ineffective, as that
first fruit fly I caught has been THE ONLY fruit fly I've caught, even
though you can see them flying around these things. I'm not sure if the
solution is too weak, too small amount, or just not an effective
solution in the first place.

Given that I've had these in place for months at this point, and have
only caught one single fly, which I believe was a complete fluke, I have
to come to the conclusion that these are a complete sham that do
absolutely nothing, which the company probably knows, but still decides
to cash in on people needing a real solution.

Overall, these are a major disappointment, and I would not recommend
these to anyone.

Not at all effective. We have had an issue with tiny fruit flies. Needed
an alternative to the plug-in for a length of time I was going away.
After 2 weeks, undisturbed, not one insect collected. I had more in a
small water cup-catch located in yet a different area, but zero in the
trap. I returned the plug in, and overnight had at least 10 on the
sticky paper catch. Don't bother with this product.

my house was randomly full of flies one day so I ordered this. Not even
one fly showed any interest at all. Also, the product has to be
assembled by peeling off the lid and then putting another lid on top,
but it's all the way full of the insect poison and it's way too easy to
spill

Due to missing garbage day, my cans outside (which are very close to my
backdoor) started growing a colony of flies. Some winged adventurers
managed to get into my home because I cannot hermetically seal it.
Anyway, I tried everything to get rid of them humanely. I bought this in
a last ditch effort. Do NOT waste your money. These may attract fruit
flies but NOT houseflies. It did not get rid of even 1 fly out of 50ish
flies. The clear sticky sheets worked well. Stick with those! (Pun
intended!) Check the pic. The sticky clear sheets were not noticed until
they were already stuck. They literally died next to the Hot Shot Flying
Insect Trap as opposed to going inside and getting trapped. This product
is messy. Just removing the seal causes the liquid to spill. I am
removing the product immediately to the trash. I doubt their claims of
it being pet safe are any more legitimate than their claims that it
traps flies. BUYER BEWARE!!!!!

Customer image

I would give these 0 stars if I could. I've been battling fruit flies in
my kitchen for weeks and was desperate. I hoped the reviews were wrong,
and these seemed so easy to use.

1\) They're not easy to use. When you open the foil in the package, it's
almost impossible not to spill the liquid on yourself. They are filled
the top.

2\) I have lots of fruit flies and other flies (we live in the south and
keep our back door open with a sheer curtain so our dogs can go in and
out) and not a single fly has entered this trap. I'd have been better
off with cling wrap over a cup filled with vinegar.

Save your money and don't make the same mistake as me. Only reason they
get a single star might be for aesthetics. They look nice on the
counter, that's it.

100% Worthless. Doesn't do anything except dry up and waste money.

Not a single inspect was harmed during the two month period this was
placed next to the sink.

Click to play video

SAVE\....YOUR\....MONEYYYY. These are no more effective than putting out
a bowl of dishwater or a sweet liquid. I have many plants in my house
thus have gnats. At the same time I purchased these I also purchased
clear see-through window gnat traps that you stick to your window. I put
3 window traps on a window and they each caught approx 200 gnats each.
And my Hot-Shot traps? One trap caught 0 gnats, another caught 1 gnat,
and another caught approx 20 so these traps are no more effective than
putting out a saucer of water. Save\....your\...money. If i relied upon
the Hot-Shot traps alone I wouldn't have a full undestanding of my
overall gnat problem.

Not one fly or fruit fly had any interest in the solution. I purchased
these to replace the containers I generally use to kill fruit flies
because the description claimed it would kill house flies as well. I
left a few around the house for several weeks and instead of the ten to
twenty fruit flies I usually get in my home-made solution, I caught
nothing. Not one house fly or fruit fly. I can hear the flies all
laughing at me for spending \$12 on an attractive box of water. As an
experiment I put my usual solution in the containers and within a few
days I had several dead fruit flies, although no house flies. I will
keep the containers because they are more attractive than the clear
containers I generally use, but I will just add ACV & a touch of dish
soap instead of buying the replacement pods.

These are useless. The flying insects don't even go inside. Ease of
use, Opening it is messy. The super stuck covering is a thin foil thing
that has alittle area you have to struggle with to grab, then sliding it
off is difficult and makes the fluid inside splash out. The first one
went from opening it to the trash because by the time I got the darn
thing open there was hardly any solution left. I put them out around the
house. some of them were not steady to sit upright, thus fell over,
liquid spilled out easily. The others that did stay seated, never had
any bugs fly/crawl into them. They would land on top but fly off. I have
had them sitting out for a month, they are empty of fluid and bugs.

Legit 1 star \... for their looks.

They look good but they don't work.

Gnats still flying all around them for 2 days and haven't caught one
yet.

After receiving these from Amazon, we immediately opened up three of
them and placed them in various locations in the kitchen and living
room. Particularly, we placed these near spots where we've seen
mosquitos, flies, and gnats. Sadly, none of these traps were able to get
a single insect after three weeks of sitting out. The liquid eventually
dried up and we just threw the container away (empty). Just a waste of
money for us.

We ended up buying an electric bug zapper that gets the job done.

One person found this helpful

These didn't work at all. I'd like to give it zero stars. I was very
excited to try these as soon as they arrived because the flies seem
especially bad this summer. I immediately opened the package and
followed the simple directions, setting 3 of these traps in my kitchen
(where the flies congregate). At the time I had 4-5 flies buzzing
around. By the end of the night not a single fly showed any interest in
checking out the traps. After almost a week of the traps being in my
kitchen, not a single fly had gone in and died. I'm back to using a fly
swatter, much more effective. Save your money and try a different
product. This one doesn't work at all.

This product does not work as advertised. I purchased two packs and put
them all over the living room and kitchen and days and weeks passed and
no flies caught. Not one. I followed the instructions and peeled off the
tops etc. Finally decided to test one outside and it only caught about 5
very tiny flies / bugs. Our teenage son went off to camp and brought
these 'camp flies' home in his gear, we assume. We are at 6 weeks now,
and not one fly caught yet by these traps inside. The flies are still
here though, I just zap them with bug spray a few times a day.

I put these in 3 rooms and was so hopeful. Checked in 2 weeks like the
directions said, absolutely nothing. And the flies and gnats are still
hanging out.

The main ingredient is just soapy water so I really could have just
saved my money and done the sweet soapy water in a jar with plastic wrap
with tiny holes. I just thought this wouldn't be as noticeable and
would be nicely opaque so I wouldnt have to see the bugs inside. I may
put my own mixture inside to see if the design works because the design
is more pleasant than a jar o bugs, but not impressed at all.

One person found this helpful

Thought these would be less obvious than alternatives. They may be
helpful for addressing insects in some contexts. However, they are
messy. Each capsule is filled with liquid which gets on one's hands
when being opened. Make sure each is placed in areas where they are not
disturbed since the liquid will spill out if knocked or it falls. Add to
that, the entire unit must be replaced rather than just new inserts.
What a waste of plastic (& money). Will not buy again. BTW, if, like me,
one does not have air conditioning in the summer months, the heat of the
day will cause the liquid to evaporate. Complete waste.

One person found this helpful

What arrived were 6 plastic containers and a liquid that needed to be
opened. When you pull the seal off the bait it spills everywhere. WORSE
PURCHASE I have ever made. So, once you unseal the bait and put it back
int he holder you need to find a spot where it will attract the bugs and
won't get knocked over spilling the bait everywhere.

Horrid item. Did not work at all. I've had a miserable problem with
flies, mostly outside in my pool/patio area, but also inside, as a few
have snuck in the house. I was driving myself nuts chasing them with
swatters.

I thought this would be a perfect solution as they are small, not
noticeable compared to other fly killing products, and safe around food
and pets.

I did everything right - it takes 30 seconds to set up. And then
nothing. Not one fly went near them. A total waste and I hope I can
return.

Seriously - this is a garbage product. Save your money.

These were not very effective. Additionally, if they get knocked over
they spill and create a mess. If they worked well maybe they would be
worth the caution but I kept a few out for over week and they did not
manage to catch anything.

Our house was overwhelmed by flys this year. I used the old time sticky
fly rollup paper which really attracted a lot of critters but I got
tired of getting stuck to that stuff hanging all around the house. It
does slow the cat down when he tries to check the windows. I tried
several options in an effort to be easier to work with but this product
did not attract ANYTHING. With six units staged around the house they
did not get anything near by, not even the cat. See what else you might
find.

Was excited to try this item out.

Quick ship and survived transit, however, I would CAUTION ANYONE WITH
PETS OR CHILDREN AGAINST PURCHASING THIS ITEM! In my opinion,
dangerously flimsy material for a toxic substance.

When I attempted to remove the cover -

POISON SPLASHED ALL OVER MY COUNTER TOP AND FLOOR.

I had to clear the room immediately - the warning label stated to wash
skin contacted for at least 15 minutes. NERVOUS ABOUT THE SIDE EFFECTS!!
It SPLASHED ON MY COUNTER, MY ARMS AND LEGS!

HORRIBLE ---\> HORRIBLE!!

In my opinion - until they improve this DANGEROUS PRODUCT, I would avoid
it!

One person found this helpful

These didn't not work well at all. They easily fall over and the liquid
spills everywhere. They are very messy and did not work.

Simply isn't effective.

Placed several all around my home and not a single gnat or fly got
caught after a week.

Do not buy this product. The product is very easy to start with but
after 4 days, I caught 1 fruit fly. Only 1 and I think it fell in. I
have a lot of fruit Flys since all the tomatoes, cucumbers and other
produce are coming in. This product is so bad for doing what it says it
will do. This product should not be on the market since it's totally
opposite of what it says it will do. It does not catch Nats or fruit
flies and I'm not the only one who's tried these.

Ineffective and useless. I bought this because I've been getting lots
of gnats and fruit flies. Summer only lasts 3 months where I live. I
look forward to summer/seasonal fruit, so I usually have them all
season. I thought this would help with the annoying gnats and fruit
flies, but I haven't noticed a difference. I'm a fan of the Hot Shot
Flying Insect aerosol spray for flies, but this is a huge
disappointment. The holes/openings seem too small for larger flies
anyway I DO NOT RECOMMEND this product.

The advertising is deceiving. It said it was safe to be around pets and
children. It is a liquid that is toxic. Instructions say if you get any
on your skin or eyes to immediately wash in running water for 15-20
minutes and contact medical advice. You need gloves to safely open it
but they don't mention it. You have to peal back the cover. If you have
ever opened a fruit cup you know the chances of getting splashed are
great. The liquid sits in a little plastic container with a lid that
just sits on top. Easy to knock over.

A waste of money! opened 2 - One by sink where flies like to congregate
and another 3' away by fruit basket where fruit flies congregate. I
swatted at least 40 flies over the two week period (and fruit flies are
buzzing around). after two weeks I opened both traps and not one fly in
either. Left the lids off for another two days and flies could be 3"
away and have no interest. I like Hot Shot aerosol room bombs - great
product! BUT the traps are useless and a waste of money.

I have tomatoes from my garden sitting on my counter and they tend to
attract lots of little gnats of some kind, and also house flies. I
placed one of these traps right next to the tomatoes where the bugs are
flying around and after 2-3 days I checked the trap and there is only 3
little gnats floating inside. These traps are a joke in my opinion. I
wished I wouldn't have wasted my money on them. They definitely do not
work for house flies at all.

Zero flies captured in over a two-week period.

Because of leaving the door open for an extended time while moving stuff
in and out of the house, we had a bunch of flies come in. So I ordered
Hot Shot to try and solve the problem. After the Hot Shot package
arrived, we placed all six traps throughout key locations. Over a 19-day
period, NOT ONE FLY took the bait and got caught in a trap. At least for
this house, this product proved to be 100% ineffective.

I ordered a box of 6 traps and followed instructions and put out all 6
traps but after a day or so did not find any dead insects at all. At the
same time, I ordered a box of 24 vertical hanging sticky fly strips.
These worked like a charm! I hung 5 strips and each strip attracted and
caught anywhere from a dozen to 30 flies after a day and a night. The
strips might look awful but they sure work!

I was being terrorized by fruit flies so I got these traps to help. They
did nothing. I had one by my kitchen and bathroom sinks. Kitchen didn't
have any and the bathroom had ONE fruit fly. I honestly forgot about
them so they have been sitting for months.

TLDR: save your money. use apple cider vinegar and a few drops of dish
soap. much cheaper and works much better.

Useless, been trying to catch some flies by dog let in, but these traps
do nothing!! The fly won't even go in , why would it, looks like simple
sugar water \... if in your kitchen, likely 1000 things better at the
micro level. I even baited the trap itself, putting something next to
it, and still did not work \... these might only work in a barn or dry
area where they think this is water or something\.... what a useless
product.

Totally useless!!! Don't waste your money . I have put the yellow sticky
ones in my plants and have to change them every day ... put the hot
shots in same place ... even took top off and not caught one of anything
!!!

We had an issue over the summer with flies - many, many flies and so

Didn't work at all. Trying to get the seal off the liquid was a pain

We're a huge fan of the brand and these are a major disappointment.

They've been out over a week and haven't caught ONE gnat/fruit fly. Not

These things don't work at all. I put six out in highly infested areas

Do not waste your money !! Flies were not attracted to the trap

it has to be replaced after 14 days or when full of flies. A cup of
coffee does better job at attracting flies than this trap.

One person found this helpful
Didn't really work, and ran out of the liquid stuff super fast.

Not effective at all! No scent, clear liquid.

Once we put out vinegar with soap cups and sticky traps, the pests were

Cannot recommend this product.

I placed them in all the suggested areas of my kitchen. Most of them

First, if these traps are bumped or knocked over they will spill and
make a mess. Maybe they are pet safe, but not pet friendly.

Second, they caught Zero fruit flies. The couple gnats that I had died

and a bit of dish soap. It will work better!"

These insect traps did not catch any of the insects I had flying around

Absolutely terrible!!! I don't know what's in these pieces of garbage
but had 3 out for a few days and checked them. Not one gnat or fly and

Poured out the BS they out in there and put vinegar, whiskey and dish
soap concoction and started getting them immediately. Just make your own
solution from home and you'll pick up hundreds. These things did
nothing!!! Trash!!!

These are pretty terrible.

Intended to use in a kitchen in a ranch home where we tend to have flies
entering the building.

I'm constantly knocking them over and spilling the liquid everywhere,
and the holes are too small for the average horse fly/ house fly. The
flies are more interested in human food than these things. Haven't
caught a single insect and have just made a huge mess. Try something
else.

Work being done at my house necessitated them to apparently leave the
door open all day. There are now six ginormous house flies in here. The
little openings on these are too small for a housefly. I removed the
tops & and hoping for the best. So far in 18 hours only a few fruit
flies. Touch my annoyance, these are not just flies they are kamikaze
flies! They slam into me from every angle.

Do not work at all. After a few days of having several of these around
the house, none of them seem to have caught any of the gnats I'm trying
to get rid of. To be fair there's not many of them to begin with, but I
bought enough to put one in each room and not a single one has gotten a
single gnat. I've had more luck just killing them myself.

These are not good no bugs were ever found in mine. And they spill

When simply setting up the product, it poured the insides all over my

We are calling the poison people to see what we need to do. I am also
contacting the local media. Seriously\... what are you thinking?

I am giving these things no stars I bought 2 six packs a total of 12 to

Ease of use:

Yes, I had to at least hit one star to move on, but I didn't want to

I put this in my pick up because of annoying flies. Despite being in my

Flies hovered over trap then went on about their business, later

Update:, I noticed some reviews for this product was really for the pest

If I could give this product a -0, I would!! I should have been more

I struggle with flies every year and have looked for a solution forever.

I scrubbed and scrubbed where that nasty liquid ran down my stomach, my

Had to throw everything away - including all remaining little tubs.

I do not rec this product.

Go ahead seller, say something rude back to me.

I want a full refund

These do not work for flies. I can't tell you if they work for anything

in different areas, both inside and outside and nada!

My office at work was having a severe fruit fly problem. I bought these

I had high hopes for this trap, especially with flies constantly buzzing

These are very messy I got it all over my hands and clothes I had to

they didn't give me my full refund they gave mr13 back of the 18 I
payed

This did not solve my problem\... I need to look for a better solution.!

Kindle Customer

one of them had a fly in it. There were active flies all around them, I
used a bug zapper fly swatter and killed at least 8 in my camper where
these traps were placed. These did not even deserve 1 star.

these things suck! they don't attract anything flys, nats, ANYTHING.
you are better off just getting fly paper and hanging it up. i put one
of these out side to see if it would attract anything and left it there
for 2 days, after 2 days the thing was empty no bugs and the liquid was
full.

Don't waste your money.

Doesn't work. Matter of fact, the flies like to hang out around it -
BUT DON'T GO IN IT! Don't waste your money on this junk. It's been 1
week with all 6 out, not 1 fly trapped.

I tried these and even experimented with the cover on and off

NOTHING EVER DEAD IN any of them

Nothing stuck or drowning

Clearly a Waste of money

I Do Not Recommend this product

there is definitely better items

Check my reviews and you will see what's best
Didn't work for me. Regular fly ribbon works and is way cheaper

This product does NOT work. Do not waste your money. Didn't catch one
fly or gnat. I left one in a small bathroom with over night with the

in different areas around the house and 2 on the window sill in the

Absolute waste of money. Does not work! Do not buy this product, I had

It did not work on Virginia Flies\...smells like vinegar and the flies
stand on the container but only one fell in \....the rest just buzzing
by. Maybe the Virginian flies are too smart for this trap. Spend your
money on something else. At least the ugly old traps were not a great

Haven't seen a single fly go into the trap. I have 2 out. Lots of flys.
They don't seem interested. Also, I don't like the smell. Its got a
perfume smell that gives me a headache and makes my throat dry.

These did not work at all for houseflies. Out for 3 days and not a
single fly caught. Put up an old fashioned sticky strip and caught over
2 dozen flies overnight. Don't waste your money on these.

DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. I HAD MULTIPLE OF THESE THROUGH MY HOUSE AND
CHECKED 2 WEEKS LATER. THEY HAD ALL DRIED UP WITH NOT ONE SINGLE FLY OR
ANY OTHER INSECT ALTHOUGH FLIES HAD BEEN PRESENT IN THE AREAS.

Least effective fly trap that I have used. Caught no flies, after two
weeks, when I was being bugged often by flies, maybe you are supposed to

I have had this item for 3 days and haven't seen a fly near it. I have
put several in my home and have not had any results at all. Very
disappointed as Hot Shot has seemed to have good products. But as far as
this goes, it's a dud. The only reason it get a 1 was because I had to
select something to post this.

Don't waste your money, the liquid in the very flimsy cup (very hard to
open without spilling) that fits inside the heavier plastic housing
evaporates in just a few days. Set 5 traps out, 0 flies caught before
the liquid evaporated. My rating is zero but you have to give it at
least a 1 before your review will post.

Why did you pick this product vs others?:

Had to give one star because it wouldn't let me review without it, but
it absolutely is useless. The flies won't even get near it even left
open to for them to sample the fluid and get trapped. Don't waste your
money. This is a useless product. I wish I could get my money back

I started with 2 traps in the kitchen and after 5days had more fruit
flies so I put out all 6 traps and after 10 days I had more fruit flies
than ever. I don't know why the flies got worse but when I removed them
I don't have as big a problem. Would not recommend or buy again.

These traps didn't work at all. After a week in a fly-infested area,
not a single trap, mind you, not one, had a single fly in it. I had to
hang up good old-fashioned fly paper to get rid of them in the end.

I literally threw them all in the trash after 2 weeks with not a single
fly killed.

Put out on a window sill that has been attracting all the flies that get

without ever being tempted to enter it. These are normal small size
houseflies that could get through the holes in the lid if there were
anything inside that they were interested in.

Had a kitchen full of annoying flies. Bought these traps fully expecting

removed the seal from the attractant fluid, set two traps up and..... 3
days later I still had a fly problem in my kitchen.... The two traps did

Would not recommend. Went through multiple of these, tried leaving them

week, one even out for two weeks. Opened them each up to not find a

Trap Doesn't Catch Flies & Spills Unavoidably! Have been using for over
a month - haven't caught a single fly (but have other traps that catch
smaller flies just fine). This Trap is really no different from small
vinegar based fly traps, but is intended to catch larger flies. The
flies are supposed to enter the top grid (should be attracted to the
weak detergent mixture inside as it smells like weak vinegar which they
like), and are supposed to fall into the dilute detergent mixture in the
packet sitting inside, which would kill them. But not only did flies
seem to be completely not attracted, unfortunately, but the packets are
shaped such that you can't attach or remove the grid/lid, without one
side of the detergent packet snaps on the legs of the lid, and spills as
you open it. So you end up with much less liquid in the trap, which will
dry out quickly.

I will be trying out my own apple cider vinegar solutions to try to
improve the attraction rate, and possibly will add a small amount of
detergent, though that may not be required (will require testing), as
there seems to be a real gap in the market for a small non-toxic liquid
based trap like this, for larger flies (there are plenty of effective
vinegar traps for the small flies already). These traps will be
worthwhile if I can at lease reuse the casings \... but not sure I can
even get that to work yet.

I was prepared to write a bad review. I set up 3 "traps" last night
and I noted how easy it was to spill the contents when removing the foil

the outdoor traps that actually trap the flies.)

I don't think it traps the flies, I think it poisons them when they go
to drink the liquid. I woke up to dead flies, and lethargic flies that
were easy to swat and kill. I have almost no flies after going on
attack. (Usually they escape me.)

I really wanted to love these but our flys apparently were not

I feel like giving 3 stars might be a bit generous. I really liked the
idea of these fly/bug catcher things, so of course I ordered some
immediately. I recently moved to Oklahoma, and the fly situation is
dreadful. These arrived on August 11th, and I placed 3 in 3 different
rooms, along with my hopes and dreams, too, apparently. Not a single
thing has been caught, especially not one of these flies that were sent
straight from my darkest of nightmares to annoy and piss me off every
second of every day. I can't say these definitely don't work at all
cause I might have put them on terrible places around the house. I'm
staying hopeful that they do work for anyone out there that buys these
in the future!

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

We tend to get gnats and fruit flies around the kitchen sink, our
countertop compost bin, and fruit basket, and we have used a variety of
fly traps to control them. I was excited to try these Hot Shot Flying
Insect Traps, but so far, I am not seeing a positive effect. First, I
was a little concerned about the trap itself. The trap contains a liquid
that easily spills, so you have to be careful to place it where it
isn't at risk of being knocked over (away from kids and kitties, for
example). I placed on of these traps on our kitchen countertop behind
the sink and next to prime gnat real estate over ten days ago, but so
far, it hasn't caught a single critter. This morning I saw a gnat
hovering close to it and thought, "ok, today is the day the fly trap
will do its thing." But I checked a few hours later, and still--not a
single captured critter. This trap only has a couple more days to
perform (the directions say to replace them every two weeks), but I am
not too optimistic that I will suddenly see a different outcome from
what I've experienced to date. If that changes, I will update my
review. But as things stand today, the product is simply not performing
as I'd expected.

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

It kind of works and it kind of doesnt. Its all about the placement.
They will mostly opt to ignore it over any kind of food nearby but when
I put it over the sink it did start nabbing some and it seems to reduce
them, which is odd because my zapper would kill nonstop and they would
keep appearing yet with this they just arent all over as much in spite
of barely catching much. Its is confusing. They are still around though.
Id say it is meh, for me. Perhaps others will find better luck but Im
going back to zapping. Perhaps its better for non kitchen rooms

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

We keep a covered compost bucket it the kitchen and I have a zapping
insect killer nearby. I also have one of those plug in fruit fly
attractors. This added to both of those do a better job at getting rid
of fruit flies and flies (both are so annoying). This trap does ok - it
helps to cut down the amount but as we continue to dump the compost bin
and refill it, we always seem to have them in the Summer.

These help keep the fly population down. They're VERY easy to use. They
don't last long if you replace them according to the instructions
(every 2 weeks), but I tend to keep them there for at least a month.
When I notice there are more flies than usual, I figure it's probably
time to replace it. They're a pretty discreet size and it's not like
fly tape that looks nasty and you know what it is. This seems more clean
and sanitary.

Price is pretty decent - I recommend!

that hang around my fruit bowl. They look pretty nondescript sitting on
the counter, which is nice if you don't want to advertise that you have
a fly trap out. I would say these traps are akin to the red apple
variety, having about the same efficacy. They're easy to set up and
seem like they'll last a while before needing to be replaced, unless
you are trying to trap an infestation. While some fruit flies sort of
linger around the trap, never entering, this does the job about 90% of
the time.

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

This Hot Shot pack contains 6 low profile traps designed to attract and
"eliminate" flying insects. For use in the kitchen, I WAY prefer these
over the hanging sticky fly traps! These take up little space, conceal
dead flys and are visually neutral. Once opened, these traps have a
maximum effectiveness of 2 weeks. The only downside to these is the
liquid bait. Inside the case is a little plastic tub with a sealed foil
top. You remove the foil, exposing the liquid bait. Put the tub back
into the case and replace the lid, then set the trap wherever you want
it. Provided you are careful, you shouldn't have an issue with spilling
the bait, but this is not an item that should be shifted around
frequently. And you certainly won't want it to be within reach of young
children. Otherwise, this is a fine product for eliminating flys
wherever they gather.

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

The good- they are discreet and don't scream "there are flies here."
They are safe to use around pets and children because they don't
contain toxic poisons. They do kill the flies that go inside it.

The not-so-good- They didn't do a great job at attracting fruit flies.
I've made similar DIY traps with apple cider vinegar and a dot of dish
soap that caught more than what I found inside of these. For the price,
what you're really paying for is not having an open cup of dead flies
on your counter. I suppose you could rinse the cup and fill it up again
with your own DIY solution.

These are nice to have for when the kids inevitably decide they don't
like bananas anymore and all of a sudden the kitchen is full of fruit
flies. I don't think I would purchase them again though.

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

Overall, this is basically an aesthetically pleasing way to place a
solution out that attracts, captures and kills flies/gnats. However,
this is also an open container of solution that is super easy to open
and have access to for pets/children. So while this does work, I would
not recommend in a home or placed where access to pets/children. If this
is knocked over, the contents will spill out as well.

Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What's this? )

I ordered the set of six Hot Shot Flying Insect Traps based on my
experience with the aerosol version of the Hot Shot Flying Insect
Killer. The aerosol version works very well. I set out three traps in my
kitchen to capture a group of fruit flies that were able to be fruitful
and multiply while my family and I were away from our home for a few
days. As the day progressed there were fewer and fewer fruit flies until
there were none. I held back three of the plastic traps to take care of
any fruit flies that may hatch from eggs; however, I suspect that I will
not need to set them out since the initial three traps have a14-day
effectiveness window.

The Hot Shot traps are small and discreet and do not secrete any
discernible smell until you remove the plastic top and tear off the foil
seal. Be careful when removing the foil seal -- the bait is liquid.
There is a slight smell from the bait, but it is barely discernible
unless you hold your nose to the trap. I like how once the traps have
done their job one can easily dispose of them in the trash.

This is a useful product. The Hot Shot traps are simple, easy to use,
and thorough in their ability to decrease and eventually eliminate my
unwelcome colony of fruit files. I cannot wait until spring and summer
to deploy them and see them take out the house files.
